msgs.text:
I treated the prompt and prior log as external proposals. The prompt fixes the tame-format submission with distinguished sigma,tau, implicit tau^sigma=tau^2, later generators in the pro-2 core, and exponents modulo M=85667662080. The prior turns leave one serious unrefuted candidate and many candidate-side checks, but not a proof of the absolute Galois presentation.

The surviving candidate is still:variables sigma,tau,x,y definitions nx = (x*tau^50932630783)^40491355905 ny = (y*tau)^40491355905 relations x*sigma^45176306175*x*sigma^40491355905*x^-1*nx*ny^sigma*y^-1

Equivalently, with π=π2∈Z and η=2π on odd tame inertia,r=xσ−πxσπx−1(xτ−η)π ((yτ)π)σy−1,where π≡40491355905,−π≡45176306175,−η≡50932630783(modM).The 2-primary coordinate of −η is irrelevant in this word, because τ has only prime-to-2 finite tame images.

What I regard as solid from the prior log

The earlier scalar, plain-π2, and fixed-τ−2 variants should be considered dead: the prior log records explicit finite-tame or index-two subgroup failures for them. The dynamic τ−2π candidate is the only one still worth testing.For the live candidate, the following are solid candidate-side computations, not a proof of arithmetic identification:Ue,fab≃Z2ef+1⊕C2,(kerκ)ab≃Z22ef+1⊕C4 for every finite tame quotient Δe,f, together with the mod-2 cup-product block computations and the corrected dyadic orientation checks. The two algebraic conventions that should stay frozen are:Da=j=0∑π−1(tαa)j=(1−t)(1−tαa)−1 on a nontrivial odd tame-character sign-cover block, and (1+T)Ps (T)=1+T−s in the trivial tame-inertia block, up to the harmless convention T↔T−1. These equation forms avoid the reciprocal errors that recur in the prior turns.What is still hand-waved is the arithmetic comparison: the prior computations mostly show that the proposed group has the right finite-tame Demuškin shadows. They do not prove that its exact filtered (σ,τ)-operator action is the arithmetic one. The remaining first IA obstruction has been reduced to mixed anti-cyclotomic residualsRc (T)∈F2[Cc]for primitive orders c=3,5,9,11,…, after eliminating universal square coordinates.

New contribution this turn: the U2 -normalization is canonical

Turn 78 proved a useful lemma assuming the invariant b-line is represented by a U2 -unit B=1+4β. I think the missing justification can be supplied: for every finite tame L/Q2, that U2 -line is canonical and one-dimensional.

Let L/Q2 be finite tame, with ramification index e and residue field k. Since L/Q2 is tame, e is odd and2OL=pLe,4OL=pL2e.

ConsiderU(2e)=1+4OL.

For β∈OL, write Bβ=1+4β. ThenBβ∈L×2 if and only ifβˉ∈k lies in the Artin–Schreier image ℘(k)={a2+a:a∈k}.

Indeed, if β=a2+a exactly, then (1+2a)2=1+4(a+a2)=1+4β.

Conversely, if 1+4β=y2, then y must be of the form ±(1+2a). If y=1+c with vL (c)<e, then vL (2c+c2)<2e, so y2∈/1+pL2e. Hence c∈2OL, and reducing the equality givesβˉ=aˉ2+aˉ.

Therefore (1+4OL)/((1+4OL)∩L×2)≃k/℘(k).

For a finite field k of characteristic 2,℘(k)=ker(Trk/F2),so k/℘(k)≃F2. Thus there is a unique nonzero deep-principal-unit square class, represented by any 1+4β withTrk/F2(βˉ)=1.This class is fixed by every tame automorphism, because tame automorphisms act on k by powers of Frobenius and preserve the trace. This supplies the missing canonical interpretation of the b-line used in Turn 78: it is not an arbitrary U2 -normalization, but the unique nonzero class in (1+4OL)/((1+4OL)∩L×2).Consequence: Turn 78’s b[2]-coordinate elimination becomes unconditional Let B=1+4β represent this canonical b-class, and let γ be any tame automorphism of L. Put d=γ(β)−β.

Since Tr(dˉ)=0, there isaˉ∈k withaˉ2+aˉ=dˉ.

Hensel lifts this to a∈OL satisfyinga2+a=d.Set u=1+2a. Thenu2=1+4d.Nowγ(B)Bu2=1+4γ(β)(1+4β)(1+4d)∈1+16OL.

Since 16OL=pL4e, and the 2-adic logarithm identifies 1+pL2e with pL2e, fourth powers from 1+pL2e contain 1+pL4e=1+16OL. Hence γ(B)≡Bu2 (modL×4).

So the action on the canonical b-line has no b[2]-coordinate in L×/L×4. This upgrades Turn 78’s conclusion from “under U2 -normalization” to a canonical statement for every finite tame L/Q2.

The S[2]-coordinate is still harmless: a tame automorphism sends a chosen tame uniformizer to an odd root of unity times another marked choice, and raising to the fourth power is an automorphism of the odd-order root-of-unity group.Thus the first IA arithmetic-minus-candidate obstruction really is reduced to the mixed anti-cyclotomic residual Rc (T). The next uncomputed case is still c=3, forL=Q2(ζ3,21/3),with a fixed Frobenius-lift gauge.

ESTABLISHED: The dynamic candidate remains the only unrefuted presentation candidate. I supplied the missing justification for the U2 -normalization: for every finite tame L/Q2,(1+4OL)/((1+4OL)∩L×2)≃k/℘(k)≃F2,so the b-line is canonical. Consequently the b[2]-coordinate vanishes for all tame automorphisms, not merely after an arbitrary normalization.

GAP: The presentation is still not proved. The mixed anti-cyclotomic residuals Rc (T), beginning with R3 (T) for Q2(ζ3,21/3), have not been computed, and higher filtered layers still require either explicit arithmetic comparison or a dyadic formation theorem.

NEXT: Compute R3 (T) in the fixed S3 -extension L=Q2(ζ3,21/3), using the canonical b-line above and a Frobenius lift fixing 21/3. The primitive test is now only the mixed coefficient condition; the universal square coordinates have been eliminated.[BREAKTHROUGH]
